entry.title: Video - Make Kubectl Actually Readable - The 2 Minute Terminal Upgrade

link.title: Video - Make Kubectl Actually Readable - The 2 Minute Terminal Upgrade

entry.title: Make Kubectl Actually Readable - The 2 Minute Terminal Upgrade

link.title: Make Kubectl Actually Readable - The 2 Minute Terminal Upgrade

entry.summary: Staring at a massive, monochrome wall of text during a kubectl describe or tracking down a failing pod status in a giant namespace is a daily frustration for anyone working with Kubernetes. When everything is the exact same shade of dull white, it is incredibly easy to miss a failing pod status, a misconfigured image tag, or a critical error event. Your eyes shouldn’t have to scan raw text lik...

entry.title: Video - Karpenter for Azure - Hands on with AKS Node Auto Provisioning

link.title: Video - Karpenter for Azure - Hands on with AKS Node Auto Provisioning

entry.title: Karpenter for Azure - Hands on with AKS Node Auto Provisioning

link.title: Karpenter for Azure - Hands on with AKS Node Auto Provisioning

entry.summary: Traditional cluster autoscaling in Kubernetes has always felt a bit reactionary. For years, platform engineers have lived in a world dictated by rigid, predefined node pools. You deploy an application, wait for a pod to fail to schedule due to insufficient resources, and then watch the Cluster Autoscaler sluggishly trigger a virtual machine scale set expansion. Minutes later, generic compute ar...
